items.title: Scope, linkage and storage duration: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this C misconception? A static local variable is globally visible because it lives for the entire program.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. A block-scope static object has static storage duration but block scope; lifetime does not widen where its name is visible.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. A block-scope static object has static storage duration but block scope; lifetime does not widen where its name is visible.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned C correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: A block-scope static object has static storage duration but block scope; lifetime does not widen where its name is visible.
A static local variable is globally visible because it lives for the entire program.

answer.explanation: A block-scope static object has static storage duration but block scope; lifetime does not widen where its name is visible.

items.title: Scope, linkage and storage duration: executable application
items.prompt: Implement a function-local static counter that increments on each call, call it n times, and print the final persistent value; print zero when n is zero.
